United Nations Security Council Resolution — Test Fixture

The Security Council, acting under Chapter VII of the Charter of the United Nations,

Reaffirming its previous resolutions on the matter and expressing grave concern at reports 
of continued violations of international humanitarian law,

Determining that the situation constitutes a threat to international peace and security,

1. Demands that all parties immediately cease hostilities and comply with applicable 
   provisions of international humanitarian law;

2. Calls upon all Member States to refrain from providing support to non-state armed groups;

3. Authorizes the Secretary-General to establish a monitoring mechanism with full and 
   unimpeded access to affected areas;

4. Decides that all parties shall allow and facilitate safe, rapid, and unimpeded access 
   for humanitarian personnel;

5. Requests the Secretary-General to report to the Security Council every 30 days on 
   implementation of this resolution;

6. Decides to remain actively seized of the matter.

TODO: This is a test fixture. Real content should be 400+ words.
